articles.title: A DJ and a Police Investigation
articles.summary: This article is about a DJ named Scott Mills who was investigated by the police. The police looked into claims that he did something wrong with a teenage boy a long time ago, between 1997 and 2000. The boy was under 16 years old. The police started looking in 2016 and talked to Mills in 2018, but they stopped in 2019 because they did not have enough proof to charge him. Recently, the BBC fired Mills from his job, but it is not clear if this is because of the police investigation. The BBC said Mills is no longer working for them. Other DJs are now doing his radio show. The news was first reported by a newspaper called the Mirror.

articles.title: A Mix-Up at the Baby Clinic
articles.summary: Laura and Beth are two moms who wanted to have babies together. They went to a special clinic in a place called northern Cyprus to get help. They picked one sperm donor so their kids would be like brothers and sisters. But when their son James was born, he looked different than expected. They got worried and did a DNA test. The test showed that both kids were not related to the sperm donor they chose. In fact, the kids were not even related to each other! This made the families very sad and confused. The BBC found other families with similar problems at clinics in northern Cyprus. These clinics are popular because they are cheap and have many donors, but they are not well controlled by laws. The families hope their story helps others be careful.

articles.title: New Rules for Food Waste in England
articles.summary: In England, new rules started that say all homes must have their food waste collected every week. This helps the environment by turning food waste into electricity instead of letting it rot in landfills, which makes bad gases. But many councils, which are local groups in charge of waste, are not ready yet. Some councils do not have the right trucks or enough money to start this service. The government gave over £340 million to help, but still, about one in four councils missed the deadline. Some councils plan to start by 2026, while others do not know when they will begin. This is a big change in recycling, and it hopes to make people waste less food too.

articles.title: Iran Attacks Israel's Oil Refinery as Trump Makes Threats
articles.summary: A war between Iran and the U.S. and Israel has been going on for a month. President Trump says Iran must agree to end the war quickly or he will destroy Iran's energy resources like power plants and oil hubs. Iran says no to U.S. demands and keeps attacking Israel, including hitting a big oil refinery in Northern Israel that made smoke. Iran also controls the Strait of Hormuz, a key waterway for oil ships, but is starting to let some ships through after talks. Trump threatens to blow up Iran's oil wells and an island called Kharg Island if the strait isn't open. He might take Kharg Island, which is important for Iran's oil exports. The situation is tense as both sides make threats and attacks.

articles.title: TSA Workers Get Paid After Long Wait
articles.summary: TSA workers who check bags at airports had to work for 44 days without getting paid. Now, they are starting to receive their back pay because the President ordered it. But many workers are still having a hard time. Some are sick or cannot pay their bills because they had no money for weeks. Airports have long lines because there are not enough workers. Angela Grana, a TSA worker from Colorado, got her paycheck today. She says it has been very tough for her and others to work without pay for so long. They had to find ways to manage with no income, which caused a lot of stress and problems.

articles.title: Big Protests in America
articles.summary: This article talks about a big protest called 'No Kings' that happened in America. People all over the country, in all 50 states, came together to march and hold signs. They were upset with President Trump and his government's rules. The protest was very large, with millions of people joining in. Organizers say it was the biggest single-day protest in U.S. history. People are frustrated about things like immigration and wars. They feel tired but hopeful that by gathering together, they can make changes. The article also mentions experts, Amy Walter and Tamara Keith, who discuss politics and what these protests mean for elections.
